questions.scenario: You're in a dungeon. The tank is at 60% HP with Regen active. A DPS just took avoidable damage and is at 45% HP. No immediate boss mechanics are coming.
questions.question: Who should you prioritize healing?
options: The tank - they always come first
Neither - Regen will handle the tank and the DPS can wait
The DPS first, then refresh Regen on tank
questions.explanation: With Regen active and no incoming damage, the tank is fine at 60%. The DPS at 45% isn't in danger. Unnecessary healing is a DPS loss - cast Glare instead and let Regen do its job.

questions.scenario: A boss is auto-attacking the tank for consistent damage. The tank is at 50% HP. You have Tetragrammaton available.
questions.question: What's the best approach?
questions.explanation: Tetragrammaton is an oGCD heal - weave it between Glare casts. This maintains your DPS while healing. Cure II costs a GCD, and Benediction is overkill at 50% HP.

questions.scenario: The boss casts a raidwide that hits everyone for 40% of their HP. Everyone is now at 60% HP. You have Afflatus Rapture and Medica II available.
questions.question: What's the optimal response?
options: Afflatus Rapture, then continue DPS - the party is fine
questions.explanation: At 60% HP with no follow-up damage, the party isn't in danger. Afflatus Rapture is a 'free' heal (builds Blood Lily) and instant. One heal is enough - overcasting wastes GCDs.

questions.scenario: You just cast Glare. The tank needs healing and you have Tetragrammaton available. Your GCD is still rolling.
questions.question: When should you use Tetragrammaton?
options: Wait for GCD to finish, then use it
Use it immediately during the GCD window
Save it for a bigger emergency
Use Cure II instead since GCD is rolling
questions.explanation: oGCDs like Tetragrammaton should be 'weaved' during the GCD window (after casting but before next GCD is ready). This lets you heal without losing DPS uptime.

questions.scenario: In a trial, both tanks are at 70% HP. Tank A has your Regen. Tank B is the off-tank with no HoTs.
questions.question: The boss is about to use a tankbuster on Tank A. What do you do?
options: Heal Tank B since they have no HoT
Pre-shield Tank A with Divine Benison
Heal Tank A after the buster hits
Use Medica II to heal both
questions.explanation: Tank A is taking the buster - they need attention. Divine Benison (shield) applied BEFORE damage reduces the buster's impact. Healing after is reactive; shielding before is proactive.

questions.scenario: The tank is at 15% HP after a tankbuster. The boss is casting another attack in 3 seconds. You have both Benediction and Tetragrammaton available.
questions.question: What should you use?
questions.explanation: At 15% with another attack coming in 3 seconds, this IS an emergency. Benediction guarantees survival instantly. Tetragrammaton might not heal enough, and Cure II takes too long.

questions.scenario: The tank is at 40% HP during normal combat. Benediction is on cooldown. Tetragrammaton is available.
questions.question: What's the best action?
options: Cure II since Bene is down
Afflatus Solace to build Blood Lily
questions.explanation: Tetragrammaton at 40% is perfect - it's free, instant, and the tank isn't in immediate danger but needs healing. Don't wait for them to get dangerously low.

questions.scenario: A DPS stands in an AoE and drops to 20% HP. The boss is targeting them with a follow-up attack. Benediction and Tetragrammaton are both available.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Benediction - save them at all costs
Let them die - it's their fault
Cure II since they're not the tank
questions.explanation: Tetragrammaton should heal a DPS from 20% adequately. Reserve Benediction for tank emergencies. DPS have less HP than tanks, so Tetra's healing is proportionally larger on them.

questions.scenario: You're 4 minutes into a fight. Benediction hasn't been used yet. The tank is at 55% HP with no immediate danger.
questions.question: What should you do with Benediction?
options: Save it for a real emergency
Use it now - it's been too long
The tank is fine, cast Glare
questions.explanation: 55% HP with no danger means no healing needed. However, holding Benediction for 4+ minutes is wasteful - you've likely missed uses. The answer here is to DPS, but be ready to use Bene proactively.

questions.scenario: Two party members are at 25% HP. The boss's next attack will hit one of them. You can only save one with instant healing.
questions.question: Who do you prioritize?
options: The one with lower current HP
questions.explanation: In an emergency where you can only save one, save the person who will actually die. The boss targeting determines who needs the heal - the other person can wait one GCD.

questions.scenario: You have 3 lilies and 0 Blood Lily stacks. The party is at full HP with no damage coming for 20 seconds.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Hold the lilies for when damage comes
Use Afflatus Rapture to avoid overcapping
Use Afflatus Solace on the tank
Convert lilies to Blood Lily for Misery
questions.explanation: At 3 lilies, you're capped and wasting generation. Even with no damage, spend a lily (Rapture for AoE overheal is fine) to avoid losing the next lily that generates in 20s.

questions.scenario: You have Afflatus Misery ready (Blood Lily full). The boss is about to jump away for 10 seconds of downtime.
questions.question: When should you use Afflatus Misery?
options: Save it for after boss returns
Use it on the boss immediately
questions.explanation: Use Misery before downtime! It's your strongest attack. During downtime you can't hit the boss, so using it after wastes potential damage. If adds spawn, use it on them.

questions.scenario: The tank needs healing. You have 2 lilies and Tetragrammaton available.
questions.question: What's the most efficient choice?
questions.explanation: Tetragrammaton is a true 'free' heal - oGCD with no GCD cost. Afflatus Solace costs a GCD. Use Tetra first; use Solace when you need to spend lilies or Tetra is on CD.

questions.scenario: You have 2 Blood Lily stacks. The boss will die in approximately 30 seconds. You have 1 lily.
questions.question: What's your priority?
options: Spend it to complete Blood Lily for Misery
Use Cure II instead to save the lily
It doesn't matter at this point
questions.explanation: With 30 seconds left, spending 1 lily + Misery cast = ~5 seconds. You have time to complete Blood Lily and cast Misery before the boss dies. A completed Misery > holding a lily.

questions.scenario: A raidwide just hit. Party is at 65% HP. You have 3 lilies and Assize available.
questions.question: What's the optimal healing response?
questions.explanation: At 65% HP, Assize alone (400 potency party heal + damage) is sufficient. Save your lilies - you're capped but one Assize handles this. Rapture would be overhealing.

questions.scenario: You're about to pull a dungeon boss. The tank is at full HP.
questions.question: What should you do before the pull?
options: Cast Glare to start DPS early
questions.explanation: Pre-pull Regen lets you start with Glare spam while the HoT handles early damage. Divine Benison is also good but Regen provides sustained value. Both is ideal.

questions.scenario: The boss is casting a tankbuster. It will hit in 4 seconds. The tank is at 80% HP.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Wait for the hit, then heal
questions.explanation: Divine Benison (shield) before the buster reduces incoming damage. Shields applied BEFORE damage are proactive and more efficient than healing after. Both Benison and Aquaveil work here.

questions.scenario: Assize just came off cooldown. The party is at 95% HP. The boss is targetable.
questions.question: Should you use Assize?
options: No - save it for when healing is needed
Wait for party to take damage first
questions.explanation: Assize deals damage AND heals. Even at 95% HP, use it on cooldown for the damage. The healing is a bonus. Holding Assize for 'better healing' loses significant DPS.

questions.scenario: You know a raidwide is coming in 8 seconds. Party is at full HP. You have Medica II and Divine Benison available.
questions.question: What's the best preparation?
options: Medica II now for the HoT
Both Medica II and Divine Benison
questions.explanation: Medica II's HoT will tick during and after the raidwide, providing sustained recovery. Pre-casting it means the party heals automatically while you DPS. Divine Benison is single-target only.

questions.scenario: You just applied Divine Benison to the tank. The shield hasn't been consumed. Another tankbuster is coming in 5 seconds.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: The existing shield will handle it
Cast Cure II to build HP buffer
questions.explanation: Shields don't stack from the same source - you can't double Benison. Since the shield is already up, add Aquaveil (15% mitigation) for additional protection. They complement each other.

questions.scenario: A heavy raidwide is coming in 5 seconds. The party is at full HP. Temperance is available.
questions.question: When should you use Temperance?
questions.explanation: Temperance provides 10% party mitigation. Use it BEFORE damage hits so the mitigation applies. Using it after means the damage was unmitigated.

questions.scenario: The boss is casting a tankbuster. Divine Benison is on cooldown. Aquaveil is available.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Aquaveil provides 15% mitigation on one target for 8 seconds - perfect for tankbusters. It's your backup when Benison is down, or use both together for big busters.

questions.scenario: You know the boss will deal 5 instances of party damage over the next 15 seconds. Liturgy of the Bell is available.
questions.question: When should you place Liturgy?
options: During the last hit for healing
questions.explanation: Liturgy of the Bell heals when damage is taken (up to 5 times). Place it BEFORE the damage sequence so all 5 hits trigger healing. Placing it late wastes stacks.

questions.scenario: Temperance is active (15 seconds remaining). A second raidwide is coming in 20 seconds.
questions.question: Will Temperance help with the second raidwide?
questions.explanation: Temperance lasts 20 seconds but has 15 seconds remaining. The raidwide in 20 seconds will hit after Temperance expires. Plan cooldown timing around mechanic timings.

questions.scenario: The tank is about to take a double tankbuster (two hits rapidly). You have both Divine Benison and Aquaveil.
questions.question: What's the optimal defensive setup?
questions.explanation: Double tankbusters are dangerous. Benison's shield absorbs some of hit 1, while Aquaveil's 15% mitigation reduces BOTH hits. Stack them for heavy tank damage.

questions.scenario: Dia has 4 seconds remaining on the boss. You're about to cast your next GCD.
questions.question: Should you refresh Dia?
options: No - wait until it falls off
No - wait until 3 seconds or less
Yes - always keep it above 5 seconds
questions.explanation: Refresh DoTs at 3 seconds or less to avoid 'clipping' (losing ticks). Refreshing at 4 seconds wastes 1 second of the previous Dia. Wait for the optimal refresh window.

questions.scenario: The party is at full HP. The tank has Regen. No mechanics are coming for 10 seconds.
questions.question: What should you be doing?
questions.explanation: When healing isn't needed, DPS. Glare is your filler GCD - spam it. Refreshing Regen early wastes ticks. Presence of Mind is for burst phases, not idle time.

questions.scenario: You need to move for a mechanic. You have Dia ready and 1 lily. Boss has 10 seconds on Dia.
questions.question: What should you use while moving?
questions.explanation: Lily heals are instant - perfect for movement. Dia isn't worth refreshing at 10 seconds remaining. Use a lily to keep your GCD rolling while moving.

questions.scenario: The boss will become untargetable in 2.5 seconds (one GCD). You have a lily and Dia has 25 seconds remaining.
questions.question: What's your final GCD?
questions.explanation: Before downtime, use your strongest ability. If Misery is ready, use it (1240 potency). If not, Glare (310 potency). Don't waste the GCD on unnecessary healing or DoT refresh.

questions.scenario: In a dungeon pull, 5 enemies are stacked. You have Holy and Glare available.
questions.question: What should you spam?
options: Glare - single target is always better
questions.explanation: Holy hits all enemies in range. At 5 targets, Holy deals 5x the effective damage of Glare. Plus Holy stuns enemies, reducing damage to the tank. Spam Holy in AoE.

questions.scenario: A party member has a debuff with a white bar above the icon. They're at 80% HP.
questions.question: What should you do?
options: Wait to see if it's dangerous
questions.explanation: White bar = cleansable with Esuna. Remove it immediately - many debuffs deal damage over time or have nasty effects. Don't wait for it to become problematic.

questions.scenario: A DPS died. You have Swiftcast available. Your co-healer (SCH) is casting Broil.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Your Swiftcast is available and the SCH is DPSing. Take the raise - Swiftcast + Raise is the standard combo. Don't wait and cause unnecessary downtime for the dead player.

questions.scenario: A raidwide just hit. Your co-healer (AST) used Celestial Opposition. Party is at 50% HP.
questions.question: What should you do?
questions.explanation: Celestial Opposition applies a HoT. At 50% with a HoT ticking, the party will recover. Adding more healing is overhealing. Trust your co-healer's abilities and DPS instead.

questions.scenario: You're progressing a savage fight. A big raidwide is coming. Your co-healer says they'll use their party mitigation.
questions.question: What should you do with Temperance?
options: Save it for the next raidwide
Use it anyway - more mitigation is better
Ask them to not use theirs
questions.explanation: Stacking party mitigations on one raidwide is wasteful - mitigation has diminishing returns. Coordinate: one healer mitigates this raidwide, the other saves for the next one.

questions.scenario: Two DPS died at the same time. You have Swiftcast. Your co-healer also has Swiftcast.
questions.question: How should the raises be handled?
options: Wait and see who acts first
questions.explanation: With two dead and two Swiftcasts available, each healer should raise one. This gets both DPS up faster than one healer doing both raises. Coordinate quickly.
